title: Berlin Ringbahn - Quick Summary
items: **The Ringbahn is one circular S-Bahn line around inner Berlin.** The S41 and S42 loop through 27 stations and take about 60 minutes for a full circle. Locals nickname the shape the Hundekopf, the dog's head.

**S41 runs clockwise, S42 anticlockwise.** They share the same track in opposite directions. Check the 41 or 42 on the front of the train, or which corner it lists as its destination, to know which way you are heading.

**It circles the centre, it does not cross it.** The Ring wraps around the historic core rather than through it, so it is not a landmark tour. For monuments in one ride, Bus 100 is better; the Ring is for reaching Berlin's neighbourhoods and understanding the city's shape.

**The whole loop is in Zone AB, and the airport is not on it.** To ride the full circle back to your start, buy a day ticket (AB, 11.20 euros in 2026); a single ticket (4.00 euros) is valid for two hours in one direction only and does not cover a round trip.

**Four corners split the ring into quarters.** Gesundbrunnen (north), Ostkreuz (east), Südkreuz (south) and Westkreuz (west) are the big interchanges. Ostkreuz is the busiest and your gateway to Friedrichshain and the East Side Gallery.

**Best stops to actually leave the train.** Treptower Park for the Soviet War Memorial, Tempelhof for Tempelhofer Feld, Sonnenallee or Neukölln for food, Schönhauser Allee for Prenzlauer Berg, Frankfurter Allee for Friedrichshain. Trains come every 5 to 10 minutes.
